content_preview.title: State AGs Urge Ninth Circuit to Keep Nexstar-Tegna Merger Frozen as Bipartisan Coalition Eyes a July 2027 Trial
content_preview.summary: Eight states defend an April injunction that bars Nexstar from integrating Tegna, while the broader 13-state coalition and Nexstar jointly propose a July 2027 jury trial.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A coalition of state attorneys general has asked a federal appeals court to leave in place a court order that keeps Nexstar Media Group's $6.2 billion acquisition of Tegna frozen, even though the deal has already closed. According to [The Desk](https://thedesk.net/2026/06/state-ags-appeals-brief-nexstar-tegna-deal/), the original eight states that filed a federal antitrust lawsuit to block the acquisition urged the appeals court to uphold a preliminary injunction issued by a lower c...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece with Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Appropriate legal precision without editorializing; the 'What We Don't Know' section honestly bounds the open questions (Ninth Circuit not yet ruled, reason for 8-vs-13 split, divestiture mechanics). Word count 975, within the News 400-1200 range.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 8 source snapshots fetched at status 200 (no archive fallbacks, no errors) and personally read from the gzipped snapshots. source-6 (Paul Weiss memo) confirms VERBATIM: April 17, 2026 PI by Chief Judge Troy L. Nunley, U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of California; Section 7 of the Clayton Act, 15 U.S.C. § 18; suit by DIRECTV, LLC + eight state AGs; ~$6.2B enterprise value; 228 broadcast stations reaching 80% of TV households in 132 local markets; March 19, 2026 DOJ/FCC clearance and close; 'separate, independently managed business unit' hold-separate language. source-2 (NY AG James) confirms VERBATIM the James quote 'Consolidating hundreds of local TV stations under one corporate owner would mean higher prices and lower quality programming for consumers' and 'integrating and consolidating their assets as part of the merger', the eight-state list, and the March 19 filing date. source-3 (CA AG Bonta) confirms VERBATIM the Bonta 'Antitrust enforcement is not political...' quote, 'a bipartisan effort', the amended-complaint quote 'is expected to create the largest broadcast station group in the United States, putting more broadcast programming in the hands of fewer people, cutting local jobs, increasing cable bills', the 13-AG expansion with the five added states (Indiana, Kansas, Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, Vermont), the April 30, 2026 amended-complaint date, and the Ninth Circuit appeal. source-0 (The Desk) confirms the appeals brief filed by the original eight states but not the five later joiners, and the limited financial-information exception. source-1 (The Desk) confirms the $6.2B figure, the proposed July 2027 jury trial in Sacramento, and the Aug-this-year-to-Feb-2027 pretrial window. source-4 (Deadline) confirms the 'a bipartisan effort' attribution and the five added Republican-leaning states. source-5 (Deadline) confirms FCC Chairman Brendan Carr and the 39% ownership-cap waiver language VERBATIM, plus the March 19 close. source-7 (TV Tech) confirms both Nexstar statements VERBATIM ('We will appeal today's decision...before the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals' and 'this pro competitive transaction will make local stations stronger and support continued investment in local journalism and fact-based news') and Judge Nunley/Ninth Circuit.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated docket number, judge name, or date. The article does not cite a docket number at all (Paul Weiss lists No. 2:26-cv-00976-TLN-CKD, but the article omits it — no fabrication risk). The April 17 PI date is correctly attributed to Paul Weiss and corroborated by the NY AG release dated April 17; TV Tech's April 18/effective-April-21 framing is a minor source-level discrepancy that does not affect the article's correctly-cited April 17 claim. All five direct quotes (1 James, 1 Bonta amended-complaint, 1 Bonta political, 2 Nexstar) are verbatim with correct attribution. CNBC (which 403s) is not cited.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Quotes from both the AGs and Nexstar are presented in balance; the Analysis section frames the legal-procedural stakes without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Nexstar/Tegna coverage exists in src/content/articles/ — genuinely new topic for the publication.
recommendations: Eight source domains (thedesk.net, ag.ny.gov, oag.ca.gov, deadline.com, paulweiss.com, tvtechnology.com) are not on config/source_allowlist.txt. This is a non-blocking warning. Consider adding the AG .gov primary-source domains and the established trade outlets to the allowlist; allowlist file left unmodified per review policy.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurately sourced News submission. Every legal specific, statistic, name, date and quote traces verbatim to a cited, non-bot-blocked source; primary court/legal facts rest on the Paul Weiss memo and the NY/CA AG press releases as specified. No provenance gaps, no fabrications, no duplicate coverage. APPROVE.
